Would Buy From New Startup: Yes, realistic if positioned as a practical workflow tool rather than infrastructure.
Why: STAiiRS is a very small founder-led China-Europe digital marketing agency with repeated multilingual content, ad copy, SEO/SEA research and client reporting workload. A new startup is acceptable if the tool saves time immediately, costs only around €100-€200/month, and does not require technical migration or trust-heavy enterprise procurement.
Recommended First Offer: Offer a 7-day hands-on trial for Baidu/Google research, WeChat/TikTok ad copy, Chinese-French-English translation and client-report drafting.

Would Buy From New Startup: Yes, realistic with a founder-to-founder sales approach.

Founder: Yuting Peng / Alex Nikonov / Ahmed Ali
Would Buy From New Startup: Possible, but only if Zwima AI helps ship features or sales workflows faster.
Why: TRAKKER is a young software startup with AI-powered lead intelligence, CRM integration and event analytics. They are startup-friendly and fast-moving, so buying from a new startup is plausible. However, because they are technical and AI-adjacent, they may prefer direct API usage or internal implementation unless Zwima AI clearly reduces time-to-market or admin overhead.
Recommended First Offer: Offer a technical pilot for lead follow-up generation, multilingual exhibitor reports and CRM enrichment using one simple API/credits account.

Founder: Yangke Sun / Yijun Chen / Hu Zhang

Why: ECENTIME has strong content, recommendation, multilingual e-commerce and customer/community workload, so the use case is real. But it appears more established, with a larger team and existing predictive/software capabilities. A new startup with no case studies may face credibility questions unless the first use is limited to non-critical content workflows.

Founder: Antong Yin / Paul Herman / Jan Tiegges
Would Buy From New Startup: Unlikely as an early paying customer unless Zwima AI offers a clear infrastructure advantage.
Why: acemate.ai has the strongest AI need and budget potential, but it is itself an AI-native product company. They likely already have direct model-provider accounts, technical staff and strong opinions about routing, costs and model quality. Without public case studies, Zwima AI may look less credible for core product infrastructure. They could still test it for internal operations, but paying early for production use is less likely.
